chapter1_listen_to_changes.title: New Paths, Familiar Land
chapter1_listen_to_changes.narrative_goal: Listen to how visitors, trade and new routes are beginning to affect daily life.
chapter1_listen_to_changes.historical_context: European explorers, missionaries and railway construction entered Kenyan communities at different times and in different ways. This prototype introduces change through local observation rather than treating it as inevitable progress.
chapter1_listen_to_changes.journal_entry: Chapter 1 - First Encounters: Newcomers and new routes could bring unfamiliar goods, beliefs and demands. Communities interpreted and responded to these changes from their own perspectives.

chapter1_observe_exchange.title: Read the Market
chapter1_observe_exchange.narrative_goal: Gather two observations about how exchange and information are changing.
chapter1_observe_exchange.historical_context: Markets and travel routes connected communities before colonial rule. New arrivals could alter existing networks without erasing the knowledge and relationships already present.
chapter1_observe_exchange.journal_entry: Exchange and Information: Communities watched how new goods, routes and messages affected relationships that already linked people together.

chapter1_deliver_message.title: A Message for the Council
chapter1_deliver_message.narrative_goal: Carry a community message to the Messenger so the wider council can discuss the changes together.
chapter1_deliver_message.historical_context: Community discussion, local authority and communication networks mattered when people faced uncertainty. Responses to colonial expansion were diverse and should not be reduced to a single story.
chapter1_deliver_message.journal_entry: Community Deliberation: When circumstances changed, communities drew on local knowledge, debate and communication to consider their responses.
